title: Retrospective Validation Fixture Corpus
description: Evidence Foundry E1 Phase 4 (FR-19/FR-20, ADR-0006). Structural de-identification boundary for the retrospective validation harness (tools/retro-validate/) -- TOOL-LOCAL BY DESIGN so no wave-2 phase touches scripts/validate-kb.mjs. E1 corpus content is restricted to synthetic + de-identified fixtures ONLY: every case MUST carry a `provenance` marker in {synthetic, deidentified} and the corpus document MUST carry a corpus-level `sourceAttestation` reference. This schema structurally FORBIDS identifier fields at the case envelope -- an enumerated denylist covering name, MRN (medical record number), DOB (date of birth), address, and contact fields is enforced by the closed `case` property set (`additionalProperties: false`: none of those keys are ever admitted, by omission, not by a hand-maintained blocklist keyword), plus a distinct SSN-like-value-pattern denylist (`not`/`pattern`) applied to every free-text case identifier/reference string, so a disguised identifier cannot ride along inside an otherwise-permitted field. The nested clinical `input` object (`$defs/clinicalInput`) is CLOSED RECURSIVELY, one level at a time, down to every leaf property it admits -- it is not just closed at its own top level: `input.patient`, `input.cbc` (including `localRanges`/`localFlags`), `input.reticulocytes`, and `input.labs` each carry their OWN `additionalProperties: false` and an explicit field whitelist mirroring the engine's own `schemas/patient-input.schema.json` (read at authoring time and copied field-for-field, not invented independently -- see `lib/identifier-denylist.mjs`'s doc comment and `tests/ef-retro-corpus.test.mjs`'s drift-detection test for how the two are kept in sync), so an identifier smuggled INTO an otherwise-permitted clinical sub-object (e.g. `input.patient.name`) is rejected by omission at that nested level too, not only at the case envelope. Real (non-synthetic, non-deidentified) patient data can never satisfy this schema -- there is no `provenance` value for it. A second, independent, procedural layer (`lib/identifier-denylist.mjs`, called by `lib/boundary.mjs#checkFixtures` immediately alongside this schema) recursively scans the ENTIRE parsed corpus document for identifier-shaped keys and PHI-marker value patterns -- including inside free-text fields this schema cannot structurally constrain (e.g. `description`, `sourceAttestation.ref`) and inside intentionally-open sub-objects (e.g. `input.symptoms`/`history`/`exam`'s wire-compatible boolean-map field names) -- so a disguised identifier riding along in prose, or under a denylisted key placed somewhere the schema's own closed-shape rules do not reach, still fails closed. A corpus validating against this schema (and clearing the denylist scan) is a STRUCTURAL claim only: it proves the fixture-corpus shape and the de-identification boundary held. It is never a clinical-validity, safety, diagnostic-performance, or IRB/DUA-compliance claim, and it never certifies the underlying real-world de-identification process (if any) that produced a `deidentified` case upstream of this repo -- that is a human/governance act (gate G3, SPIKE-007), not something this schema, or any software, can attest to.

schemaVersion.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Integer fixture-corpus-document-shape version, bumped only when this schema's own shape changes.

corpusId.description: Stable slug identifying this corpus (e.g. in run-provenance.json / access-log.jsonl corpus-level references, FR-21/FR-22). Never a patient identifier.

description.description: Free-text human summary of what this corpus covers. Authors MUST NOT place any patient-identifying detail here. This schema cannot itself parse free prose for PHI content, so it is ALSO scanned by the loader-level procedural denylist (`lib/identifier-denylist.mjs`) for identifier-shaped markers (e.g. "MRN:", "DOB:", "Patient Name:", SSN-/phone-/email-shaped values) -- a documented human obligation backstopped by an automated, fail-closed structural check, not a schema-only guarantee.

cases.description: Every element MUST validate against `$defs/case`, in particular its `provenance` requirement and closed identifier-free property set.

sourceAttestation.description: Corpus-level source attestation (FR-20): where this corpus's content came from, at a reference-only granularity. `ref` is a citation/pointer (e.g. a synthetic-generation note id, or an external partner's DUA/IRB document id per SPIKE-007) -- NEVER patient-identifying content itself.

ref.description: Attestation reference/citation id. Structurally rejected if it contains an SSN-like value (###-##-####); also scanned by the loader-level identifier-denylist for other PHI-marker patterns.

provenanceClass.description: Corpus-wide provenance class. E1 admits no other value -- there is no 'real'/'identified' option.

deidentificationStandard.description: Populated only when `provenanceClass` is `deidentified`; the named de-identification standard (HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination) the upstream, out-of-repo process claims to have followed. This field records a CLAIM about upstream process, not a guarantee this schema enforces or can enforce.

case.description: One fixture case. Closed property set (`additionalProperties: false`): `name`, `patientName`, `mrn`, `medicalRecordNumber`, `dob`, `dateOfBirth`, `address`, `contact`, `contactInfo`, `phone`, `email`, `ssn`, `socialSecurityNumber`, or any other identifier-shaped key is rejected by omission -- it is simply not in the allowed key set below, not by a hand-maintained negative list. `caseId`/`sourceRef` additionally reject any SSN-like value pattern, independent of key name.

caseId.description: Stable case identifier local to this corpus. Never a patient identifier -- structurally rejected if it contains an SSN-like value.

provenance.description: REQUIRED per-case provenance marker (FR-20). No 'real'/'identified' value exists in this enum -- there is no way to mark a case as real patient data and have it validate.

sourceRef.description: Optional pointer into the corpus-level `sourceAttestation` (e.g. a sub-batch id). Structurally rejected if it contains an SSN-like value.

tags.description: Free-form regression-lane tags (e.g. `dangerous-miss-regression` for the P4-T8 E0 adapter output).

clinicalInput.description: Deterministic clinical facts only -- mirrors the closed top-level key set of schemas/patient-input.schema.json (itself already additionalProperties:false, no free-text identifier fields, no PHI by construction). Any key outside this exact set -- in particular any direct or indirect identifier field -- is structurally rejected here, one level before the harness ever reaches the engine's own patient-input validation (P4-T3). Every nested sub-object below (`clinicalPatient`, `clinicalCbc`, `clinicalReticulocytes`, `clinicalLabs`, and `clinicalCbc`'s own `localRanges`/`localFlags`) is ALSO closed (`additionalProperties: false`) with its own explicit field whitelist mirroring `schemas/patient-input.schema.json`'s corresponding sub-shape field-for-field -- so an identifier smuggled into a nested clinical object (e.g. `input.patient.name`, `input.cbc.mrn`) is rejected at THIS boundary, not only detectable one layer downstream by the engine's own validation. `symptoms`/`history`/`exam` remain intentionally wire-compatible boolean maps (open field names, `$defs/booleanMap` -- unchanged from `schemas/patient-input.schema.json`'s own SPIKE-003 RQ4 decision) because their VALUES are constrained to booleans/tristate enum strings only and can never carry free-text identifier content; the loader-level `lib/identifier-denylist.mjs` scan independently catches an identifier-shaped KEY placed there (e.g. `history.patientName`) as a second, procedural layer this schema's `additionalProperties` cannot reach without closing off legitimate open-vocabulary clinical fact names.

clinicalPatient.description: Mirrors schemas/patient-input.schema.json#/properties/patient field-for-field. No name/DOB/MRN/contact field exists in this whitelist.

clinicalCbc.description: Mirrors schemas/patient-input.schema.json#/properties/cbc field-for-field, including its nested closed localRanges/localFlags sub-objects.

clinicalLabs.description: Mirrors schemas/patient-input.schema.json#/properties/labs field-for-field. No name/DOB/MRN/contact field exists in this whitelist.

clinicalSmear.description: Mirrors schemas/patient-input.schema.json#/properties/smear -- a closed enum-of-strings array, never free text.

booleanMap.description: Mirrors schemas/patient-input.schema.json#/$defs/booleanMap verbatim. Wire-compatible boolean fact map: callers may continue sending bare JSON booleans or send the SPIKE-003 tri-state strings true, false, or unknown. Field names remain open here (same SPIKE-003 RQ4 decision the engine's own schema makes) -- but every VALUE is constrained to a boolean or a 3-value enum string, so this openness can never carry free-text identifier content. An identifier-shaped KEY placed here (e.g. `history.patientName`) is caught by the loader-level `lib/identifier-denylist.mjs` scan, not by this schema.

referenceLabels.description: Human-authored expected-output labels this case will be compared against for software-agreement metrics (FR-21, OQ-5) -- not present until a corpus author supplies them; entirely optional at the schema level.
